reviews.review_description:
I picked this up the other day and am on the fence about whether or not I should return it. The world and story are intriguing, the graphics are mostly great (although putting a sheen on rock or other traditionally dull textures just to show off dynamic lighting seems a bit self-sabotaging), and the puzzles are challenging.

However, I'm not sure I can see past just how terrible the locomotion is. First off, there's no reason this game couldn't have been completed using smooth locomotion. However, knowing processing limitations, etc, I understand why the devs made it teleport-only. 

What I truly can't comprehend is why a consistently functional teleport system could have been used instead of the current system, which barely works. I will point to an area, see the yellow silhouette, even wait until the little footsteps materialize, release to it...nothing. This happens more frequently at locations that are right in front of a puzzle,which, of course, makes it difficult to solve puzzles. No matter what I do, I still can't arrive at certain locations consistently. I'd say that im able to successfully move to puzzle locations about 30% of the time. It doesn't matter which controller stick I use. There are some locations (such as the "up-close" location at the sphere with the kerosene lamp in room 3) that I cannot move to at all, no matter what I do. I had to reach past my headset boundary to toss the burning twig into the lamp because it wouldn't allow me to access the close, crouching position that is needed to light the fire.

I really wanted to like this game, but the teleportation is so bad that it makes playing the game virtually impossible and ruins an otherwise enjoyable escape room experience. I will be returning it.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for the feedback, truly greatful you took so much of your time to explain the issue. 
My initial guess is that you're not releasing the joystick quick enough - but instead maybe slowly letting it back to it's neutral position. The game listens for a "sudden release" of the joystick. I'm not trying to defend the method I developed, it has to change obviously. 
Since I'm a solo dev, the reason for not having smooth movement was time. The game was first developed for mobile, and with that the same teleportation movement you see in this VR-version. 

Anyway, thank you so much again for the feedback. Will take this into account for the next patch!
David

reviews.review_description: I’m really enjoying Legacy – Reawakening. The world is absolutely beautiful and once you’re in, you feel completely pulled into it. The puzzles are different from the usual games, which makes it feel fresh and interesting the whole way through. It’s clearly made with care. Honestly, it feels like it should cost more than it does. I don’t think anyone would regret buying this one.

reviews.review_title: Great puzzle game with stunning graphics
reviews.review_description:
I love this game, especially after having started it on Android! Now that it's an immersive VR game, it's even better. 
The puzzles are logic and perspective based, making them somewhat challenging, but not overly complicated. Combine that with the fact that many puzzles are connected between 2 or 3 rooms, and you've got a great adventure of puzzle solving.
Besides fun puzzles, the graphics are amazing: Beautiful underground rooms with an Egyptian feel to them, and lighting effects that are commendable. 
The movement style is point and click, not one of my favorite movement types but the other aspects of the game make up for it.
Legacy - Reawakening is truly a hidden gem in the VR gaming realm that should not be overlooked!

I can’t believe the price for this game - it is worth so much more!  My husband and I have been playing for hours, and are amazed at how many rooms and puzzles there are to solve.  If you like The Room and solving puzzles, you will love this game.

reviews.review_title: Good Long Game but has Issues
reviews.review_description: I really love puzzle, narrative games like this one. But the way you get the last hint for every puzzle is annoying. It brings up the browser and a youtube video. Totally brings you out of the game. And I wish there was a more logical way to go from one puzzle to the next instead of moving back and forth to rooms.

I haven't finished this game yet,  but I've been playing for a couple of months and I agree with the other reviewers that say this game goes on and on. Every time I think I'm getting close to wrapping it up,  I enter a new,  very large level.  Most of the puzzles are logical, and I'm the type of person who will spend hours trying to get unstuck in a game before finally resorting to a hint. I really don't like to use hints,  but I think I've used four so far,  and with two of them I sort of kicked myself for not seeing the solution.  But on two of the hints,  I don't think i ever would've figured them out.  I finished The Room VR before starting this game,  and although that game had cool hand tracking and this game does not,  this game is at least 3X bigger

The graphics in this puzzle game are truly amazing! Each new room I get to I need to stop and just look around at the beauty of it. I just got into room 10 and needed to stop and tell the developer What an amazing job you’ve done. Granted, this is not, for me, an easy puzzle game. I’m so thankful for the excellent hint system. OK, back to it!
Just finished, fantastic! Have to mention the great music and sound effects. Can’t wait for your next endeavor David! Thank you!!
Excellent adventure, i spent 7 hours in the game. There must be dozens of different rooms and scores of different games, i particularly like the crows. good fun.

I love puzzle-solving games. Sometimes they're disappointing, sometimes they're fun. But this game is perfect and has wrapped the puzzles in a beautiful, inspiring setting. You're challenged to look closely and pay attention to the details. And every time you make progress, it feels like a reward. The atmosphere, music, and ambiance fit together perfectly. Good for hours and hours of play. I'm surprised that I, and many others, haven't noticed this game before. If you like puzzle-solving games, buy it now. The puzzles are sometimes a bit difficult; you have to see the logic, and that's why they're so good. Well done. Thank you, developer, for creating this game.

This has to be the best value puzzle game in Meta!! 
The shading depth of graphics is superb, definitely have admin the effort that’s been put into the making of the game by the Dev..👏🏻🌞👌💯🥂
The Hint system is perfect others please note.!
The puzzles, well..🙈 grrrrr..lol 
I’ll get here in the end, I know where I should be,  but can I blooming get there. Seriously looking forward to the next venture..well done 🍻🍻🌞

Just finished this game and it was a true delight. At first I thought it would be off-putting  with no free roam capability, but I quickly got used to the node-based movement system and it became perfectly natural. This game is packed full of innovative puzzles that range from straightforward to quite challenging, but they were never ridiculously difficult or esoteric. Which is great. The game also has one of the best hint systems I've ever seen, with the perfect level of increasing insight.  I'll rank this one up there with the best puzzlers on the platform. Bring on the sequel!  Well done, sir!

reviews.review_title: Great potential but needs some polish
reviews.review_description:
I bought the game after playing the free trial to support the developer but I'm not going to continue playing it just yet as it needs some quality of life improvements and bug fixes.

This game has great potential, but a few issues are holding it back.

The spatial audio is glitchy.  Sound effects come from the wrong directions.  It's possible that the game wasn't tested with natural turning and the developer assumed the player would always be facing the same direction. Standing support should always work with natural turning.

Object interactions can be really finicky, especially moving levers. There's no hand presence in the game, everything is point and click, so maybe having the thumb stick pull levers could improve the interface.

Wish list items:

I wish the game supported hand presence both to increase immersion and to make interactions more natural, but this would likely require a major overhaul of the code. If the point & click interface gets polished that'll be enough.

Spoken dialogue. I realize that this could be expensive for an indie developer but I'd be perfectly happy with AI voices, (OCD nerds would likely flip out though).

Real time lighting. Everything looks sharp on the Quest 3 but the lack of real time lighting and shadows makes the world feel a bit static and artificial.

developer_response.body: Fantastic feedback, thank you so much!

reviews.review_description: It is very hard to find such a quality game with so many different challenges and rooms to explore at such a great price. I am very picky about the games I play and love puzzles and this one goes on and on and on where other puzzles at a much higher price end too quickly. Congratulations to the Developers for creating such a interesting game at a decent and affordable price. I am currently on room number 9 and I am anxious to see what lies ahead. I am also excited to check out your other games after seeing the type of quality you put into your games. Thank you

developer_response.body: Oh this makes me so happy to read. I'm making these games myself so it truly means a lot to hear that someone likes them ❤️

reviews.review_title: Lov it and does it ever end? Hope NOT 🤩
reviews.review_description: I reviewed game after first room and just wasted time by just telling u details about it So instead I’m saying JUST BUY IT ,🤩 I hav never been a fan of 2 things that this game has n it, that is point and click and teleportI! full sprint right by and never give it as much as a look! BUT I was proven wrong today and hands down is the most challenging, beautifully detailed and mysterious and I  was shocked at the price especially after I really got started!! I’m on room 17 and aint even close to being done so hours hav been spent on this one and the best vr adventure escape mystery one yet! Story is great controls great and HAS HINTS! A true GEM of a game plz get it u won’t regret it! I’m a fan of this developer and can’t wait to c what comes next from them either! They truely hav a passion and obviously doing what they were born to do!!! Rare gem of a game to say the least!!

developer_response.body:
Wow thank you so much❤️
As a solo developer it means so, so much to read this. Glad you liked the game!
